daily_fact: Time Out reports that viral New York City shop I’m donut? will offer $1 donuts next week, giving locals a cheap, cheerful treat.
topline_md: New York’s biggest updates span housing, labor, transit and immigration: lawmakers moved to ease shelter access and strengthen ICE protections, 32BJ workers reached a deal averting a strike, and federal funding for the Second Ave. Subway was restored.

items.title: Second Ave. Subway funding restored before court hearing
items.why_it_matters: Restored federal money keeps a major transit expansion moving for East Harlem and the wider subway system.

items.title: 32BJ building workers reach deal, averting NYC strike
items.why_it_matters: Avoiding a strike prevents disruptions in apartment and office building services across the city.

items.title: Bill would ease proof requirements for homeless NYC families seeking shelter
items.why_it_matters: The change could speed shelter access for families in crisis and reduce barriers at intake.

items.title: Mamdani plans 6,500 curbside trash bins citywide
items.why_it_matters: Replacing parking with containerized trash storage could reshape streets and reduce rats and sidewalk garbage.

items.title: Hochul proposes stronger protections for New Yorkers from ICE
items.why_it_matters: New state limits on immigration enforcement could affect policing, public services, and immigrant families citywide.

items.title: City used troubled contractors for winter snow removal
items.why_it_matters: Questions about contractor oversight could influence street safety and emergency response during future storms.

items.title: Allies urge veto of protest buffer zone bill
items.why_it_matters: The fight could change rules for demonstrations and public access around contentious city sites.

items.title: DOT unveils new 31st Street bike lane redesign
items.why_it_matters: The Queens redesign could affect traffic safety, parking, and commuting for nearby residents and businesses.

items.title: MTA debuts new bus simulator for driver training
items.why_it_matters: Better driver training could improve bus safety and service reliability for New York riders.

items.title: Bronx Week 2026 schedule announced through July 11
items.why_it_matters: Residents can plan for major borough-wide cultural events, parades, and community gatherings over several months.

items.title: Free Earth Day events planned across New York City
items.why_it_matters: Families can access free local activities tied to parks, sustainability, and neighborhood engagement this week.

items.title: Lincoln Center revives free summer festival
items.why_it_matters: The festival offers accessible outdoor arts programming for New Yorkers seeking free summer entertainment.

items.title: Midtown WeWork building may become apartments
items.why_it_matters: A major office-to-housing conversion could add residences and reshape Midtown’s housing and street activity.

items.title: Hell's Kitchen rooftop farm expands fresh food access
items.why_it_matters: The project supports local food distribution and community agriculture in a dense Manhattan neighborhood.

items.title: Brooklyn's Mister Sunday releases 2026 outdoor party lineup
items.why_it_matters: The popular outdoor music series draws crowds and shapes summer nightlife plans for many residents.
